text.title: Rate Limiting Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Rate Limiting methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare algorithm per endpoint class from {token_bucket, leaky_bucket, fixed_window, sliding_log, sliding_window_counter}; mixed per-endpoint algorithms without class mapping are forbidden.

rationale.source: Rate-limiting taxonomy; Stripe API rate-limit blog

rule.rationale: Algorithm choice has implications for burst behaviour; per-class declaration makes the trade-off explicit.

rule.statement: Key strategy MUST be declared from {user_id, tenant_id, ip, api_key, composite}; per-IP keys MUST account for NAT (use X-Forwarded-For with trusted-proxy list).
rule.rationale: Wrong key throttles innocent neighbours (office NAT) or fails to throttle the attacker (rotating IPs).

rule.statement: Every Redis limit key MUST carry an explicit TTL matching the window; raw counters without TTL are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without TTL, limit keys accumulate forever; Redis OOMs on its own throttle counters.

rule.statement: 429 responses MUST include `Retry-After` (seconds or HTTP-date) and `RateLimit-Remaining` / `RateLimit-Limit` headers; bare 429 without Retry-After is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without Retry-After, well-behaved clients retry instantly; the API stays under attack from its own clients.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare a bypass list (health checks, internal metrics, admin paths) so the limiter does not block monitoring; bypass paths MUST be matched exactly, not by prefix.
rule.rationale: Blocking /healthz with a per-IP limit takes the service down via its own load balancer health checks.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare fail-mode (open or closed) for when the limit store is unreachable; un-declared fail mode is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent fail-open lets attackers bypass during Redis outages; silent fail-closed turns Redis brownouts into full outages. The choice must be a deliberate decision.

rule.statement: If algorithm supports burst (token_bucket), burst size MUST be declared per class; default to 2x sustained rate.
rule.rationale: Implicit burst sizing causes surprise: too small breaks legitimate clients, too large defeats throttle goal.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
